cases.prompt_body: Mission control room with a broad multi-screen front wall and seated operator silhouettes below it, most screens show calm telemetry-like imagery or camera feeds without readable text, only one upper screen carries a diffuse smoke-trace pattern echoing the Challenger failure, the room remains procedural and specific, pale green monitor glow, no giant plume, no graph-wall hero, no exterior launch view

Three-quarter mission control interior with multiple mission-status screens across the wall, one isolated screen or sub-panel shows a soft smoke-bearing feed while the surrounding displays remain ordinary and text-clean, operators stay secondary, consoles and annunciators remain technical but unreadable, no cracked display, no giant plume dominating the room, procedural calm under tense room light

Institutional command room with a normal telemetry wall made of multiple monitor blocks, only one monitor carries a restrained plume-like failure signal and the rest stay routine, seated staff remain nearly still below it, mission-control identity stays clear, no exterior stack, no fireball, no mushroom-cloud read, no broken-screen metaphor, restrained failure awareness
